mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals weakening labor demand and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 55% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer and housing demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint decline periods show no significant return differential versus baseline periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production index increase above 0.5 percent signals robust manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during post-shock recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid monthly CPIAUCSL acceleration above 0.3 percent signals unexpected inflation shocks that compress real equity valuations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns dominate triggered months with accuracy exceeding 60%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and corporate revenue potential, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 3.0 percent combined with retail sales expansion signals strong aggregate demand, but in a late-cycle expansion this triggers profit-taking and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict negative returns during late-cycle phases with accuracy below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production decline exceeding 0.4 percent alongside falling housing starts indicates synchronized supply and demand weakness, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in more than 60% of triggered instances.
